prompt:
I run receiving at the Two Rivers fruit co-op and settlement week is still three spreadsheets and an argument. The delivery tickets are already in code (Models.cs — CrateLine and Delivery, don't change them); what I need is the reporting layer the settlement app calls. C# / .NET 10, one project, and I want this written as LINQ pipelines over the ticket stream — grouped, aggregated, explicitly ordered — not foreach-and-mutate soup.

Add a static class CoopReport (namespace OrchardSales) plus these sealed records: VarietyTotal(string Variety, int Crates, decimal Revenue), GrowerSummary(string GrowerId, decimal Revenue, int Deliveries, int DistinctVarieties), DailyTotal(DateOnly Day, decimal Revenue, string TopVariety), PriceSpread(decimal Min, decimal Max, decimal WeightedAverage). Records because the tests compare results by value.

Ground rules: a line is worth Crates * PricePerCrate; a ticket's revenue is the sum of its lines; a ticket with no lines (everything graded out) is worth zero but still counts as a delivery. Never mutate the input. Every list you return must have a deterministic, explicitly specified order — I diff these reports run over run.

CoopReport.VarietyTotals(IEnumerable<Delivery>) -> IReadOnlyList<VarietyTotal>: flatten every line off every ticket, group by variety, total crates and revenue per variety. Order: revenue descending, then variety name ascending with ordinal comparison. Empty input -> empty list.

CoopReport.GrowerLeaderboard(IEnumerable<Delivery>, int top) -> IReadOnlyList<GrowerSummary>: per grower — total revenue, number of tickets (empty tickets included), count of distinct varieties they delivered. Order revenue descending, then GrowerId ascending ordinal; return only the first `top`. top of 0 -> empty; negative top -> ArgumentOutOfRangeException; top past the end -> everyone.

CoopReport.DailyTotals(IEnumerable<Delivery>) -> IReadOnlyList<DailyTotal>: one row per calendar day that has at least one ticket, ascending by day. Revenue is the day's total; TopVariety is the variety with the most crates that day, ties broken by variety name ascending ordinal. A day where every ticket graded out (zero crates anywhere) reports TopVariety as the empty string.

CoopReport.SpreadFor(IEnumerable<Delivery>, string variety) -> PriceSpread: over every line of that variety — lowest per-crate price, highest per-crate price, and the crate-weighted average price (total revenue over total crates) rounded to 2 decimal places with Math.Round midpoints-away-from-zero (growers get the benefit at the midpoint, that is settlement policy). No line of that variety anywhere -> InvalidOperationException with the message exactly: no crates of variety 'Mutsu' (for variety Mutsu — same shape for any name).

CoopReportTests.cs is the acceptance contract — it pins the exact rows, the tie-breaks, and the rounding. Don't touch the tests, the csproj, or .gitignore. Done means `dotnet test` is green.
